name: groundcover API
what_it_does: eBPF-based full-stack Kubernetes observability platform with REST API that automatically collects logs, metrics, and traces from all services without code instrumentation, powered by in-cluster ClickHouse storage.
use_cases: Querying application performance metrics without modifying service code
Correlating logs, metrics, and traces in a unified query interface for incident agents
Detecting K8s network topology and service dependencies automatically via eBPF
Setting up automated alerts and runbook triggers based on SLO breaches
Accessing historical performance data stored in-cluster for cost-efficient long-term retention
not_for: Lightweight observability with minimal resource overhead (eBPF + ClickHouse adds cluster overhead)
Organizations without Kubernetes expertise to manage the in-cluster stack
best_when: An agent needs comprehensive Kubernetes observability (logs + metrics + traces) without vendor lock-in or per-host agent pricing.
avoid_when: You run mixed K8s and non-K8s workloads and need unified observability across both.

auth.notes: API key from groundcover cloud portal. Token-based authentication for all REST API calls. In-cluster queries also available via kubectl port-forward.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for up to 2 nodes; unlimited data retention in-cluster
pricing.notes: In-cluster data storage means no per-GB cloud ingestion fees. Cost model is per-node rather than per-volume. Can be significantly cheaper than Datadog at scale.

performance.rate_limits: Determined by in-cluster ClickHouse capacity; no external rate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Documentation is growing but not as mature as established observability platforms. REST API is newer and less documented. Primary interface is the groundcover web UI.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All data queries are idempotent. Alert creation endpoints support idempotent operations.
known_agent_gotchas: REST API documentation is limited — much functionality is UI-only
ClickHouse in-cluster requires substantial memory (4GB+ per node)
Service mesh detection relies on eBPF kernel version compatibility
Newer product with less third-party integration ecosystem than Datadog/New Relic

security_score_components.security_notes: In-cluster data storage means observability data never leaves your environment. eBPF access requires kernel privileges — verify security implications for your threat model. SOC2 certified.
